comment_text.ud5.2:1.5: Here I use “Blessed One” rather than my usual “Buddha” for bhagavā. Normally the difference is immaterial, as it is simply the most common way to address the Buddha. In this case, however, the passage Ānanda is referring to uses bodhisatta, and it is quite striking that he changes it here.
comment_text.ud5.2:2.1: The Buddha switches back to bodhisatta, and depersonalizes it by using the plural.

root_text.ud5.2:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud5.2:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā ānando sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.2:1.5: Yāva appāyukā hi, bhante, bhagavato mātā ahosi, sattāhajāte bhagavati bhagavato mātā kālamakāsi, tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapajjī”ti.
root_text.ud5.2:2.1: “Evametaṁ, ānanda, appāyukā hi, ānanda, bodhisattamātaro honti.
root_text.ud5.2:2.2: Sattāhajātesu bodhisattesu bodhisattamātaro kālaṁ karonti, tusitaṁ kāyaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.ud5.2:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud5.2:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud5.2:1.3: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Ānanda came out of retreat and went to the Buddha. He bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.ud5.2:1.5: How short-lived was the Blessed One’s mother! For seven days after the Blessed One was born, his mother passed away and was reborn in the host of joyful gods.”
translation_text.ud5.2:2.1: “That’s so true, Ānanda! For the mothers of beings intent of awakening are short-lived.
translation_text.ud5.2:2.2: Seven days after the beings intent on awakening are born, their mothers pass away and are reborn in the host of joyful gods.”
translation_text.ud5.2:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud5.2:4.2: all depart, leaving the body behind.
translation_text.ud5.2:4.3: The skillful, understanding that all is lost,
translation_text.ud5.2:4.4: would keenly practice the spiritual life.”

variant_text.ud5.2:2.1: Evametaṁ, ānanda → evametaṁ ānanda evametaṁ ānanda (sya-all)
